title: Macrotyloma bieense
taxonomy: Macrotyloma bieense is a species of legume in the family Fabaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Macrotyloma, which comprises several species of herbaceous plants. The species is further classified within the subfamily Faboideae, which includes a wide range of legumes. Macrotyloma bieense is native to the Indian subcontinent and is known for its ability to thrive in a variety of environments. Its classification reflects its botanical relationships and characteristics, which are typical of the Fabaceae family.

intro: Macrotyloma uniflorum, commonly known as Macrotyloma bieense, is a leguminous plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine and has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in managing various health conditions such as diabetes, cardiovascular diseases, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Macrotyloma bieense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
